---
title: "MA641 Research Project(Seasonal)"
author: "Yash Tanaji Bhosale"
date: "2025-11-23"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)

# Load the CSV (replace with your correct filename/path)
cpi_raw <- read.csv("CPIAUCSL.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert date column (MM/DD/YYYY)
cpi <- cpi_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Date = mdy(observation_date),   # FIX: use mdy() not ymd()
    CPI  = as.numeric(CPIAUCSL)     # ensure numeric
  ) %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%
  select(Date, CPI)

# Inspect
str(cpi)
head(cpi)
tail(cpi)

# Plot CPI
ggplot(cpi, aes(x = Date, y = CPI)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "U.S. CPI  — Monthly",
    x = "Date",
    y = "CPI Index"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
## STEP 2A: Descriptive Statistics ----------------------------------------

library(moments)

mean_cpi  <- mean(cpi$CPI, na.rm = TRUE)
var_cpi   <- var(cpi$CPI, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_cpi    <- sd(cpi$CPI, na.rm = TRUE)
skew_cpi  <- skewness(cpi$CPI, na.rm = TRUE)
kurt_cpi  <- kurtosis(cpi$CPI, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Mean:", mean_cpi, "\n")
cat("Variance:", var_cpi, "\n")
cat("SD:", sd_cpi, "\n")
cat("Skewness:", skew_cpi, "\n")
cat("Kurtosis:", kurt_cpi, "\n")

```
```{r}
## STEP 2B: Histogram + Density -------------------------------------------

ggplot(cpi, aes(x = CPI)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins = 50,
                 fill = "lightblue",
                 color = "black",
                 alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "red", size = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Histogram & Density of CPI",
       x = "CPI Index",
       y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
## STEP 2C: Rolling Mean & Variance ---------------------------------------

library(zoo)

cpi$roll_mean <- rollmean(cpi$CPI, k = 12, fill = NA, align = "right")
cpi$roll_var  <- rollapply(cpi$CPI, width = 12, FUN = var, fill = NA, align = "right")

# Plot rolling mean
ggplot(cpi, aes(x = Date, y = roll_mean)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "12-Month Rolling Mean – CPI",
       y = "Rolling Mean") +
  theme_minimal()

# Plot rolling variance
ggplot(cpi, aes(x = Date, y = roll_var)) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "12-Month Rolling Variance – CPI",
       y = "Rolling Variance") +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
## STEP 2D: ADF & KPSS tests ----------------------------------------------

library(tseries)
library(urca)

# ADF test
adf_cpi <- adf.test(cpi$CPI, alternative = "stationary")
adf_cpi

# KPSS test (Level stationarity)
kpss_cpi <- ur.kpss(cpi$CPI, type = "mu")
summary(kpss_cpi)

```

```{r}
## STEP 3: Make CPI stationary (Log + differencing + seasonal differencing) ----

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tseries)

# 3.1 Log transform
cpi$log_CPI <- log(cpi$CPI)

# 3.2 First difference (remove long-term trend)
cpi$diff_log_CPI <- c(NA, diff(cpi$log_CPI, lag = 1))

# 3.3 Seasonal difference (lag = 12)
cpi$season_diff <- c(rep(NA, 12), diff(cpi$log_CPI, lag = 12))

# 3.4 Combined difference: (d=1 AND D=1)
# log(CPI)
cpi$diff_season_diff <- c(rep(NA, 12), diff(cpi$diff_log_CPI, lag = 12))

# Clean dataset: remove NA rows
cpi_diff <- cpi %>% 
  filter(!is.na(diff_season_diff))

# 3.5 Plot the twice-differenced series
ggplot(cpi_diff, aes(x = Date, y = diff_season_diff)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Differenced & Seasonally Differenced Log CPI",
    y = " log(CPI)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 3.6 ADF test on transformed CPI
adf_cpi_transformed <- adf.test(cpi_diff$diff_season_diff, alternative = "stationary")
adf_cpi_transformed

```

```{r}
## STEP 4: Seasonal ACF & PACF for CPI ------------------------------------

library(forecast)

# Use the fully differenced series
y_cpi <- cpi_diff$diff_season_diff

# 4.1 ACF (show up to lag 36 to see seasonal spikes)
Acf(y_cpi, lag.max = 36, main = "ACF + Seasonal  CPI")

# 4.2 PACF
Pacf(y_cpi, lag.max = 36, main = "PACF + Seasonal  CPI")

```

```{r}
library(forecast)
library(dplyr)

y <- cpi$CPI

# Fit all candidate models ---------------------------------------------

fit_112_011 <- Arima(y, order=c(1,1,2), seasonal=list(order=c(0,1,1), period=12))
fit_212_011 <- Arima(y, order=c(2,1,2), seasonal=list(order=c(0,1,1), period=12))
fit_111_111 <- Arima(y, order=c(1,1,1), seasonal=list(order=c(1,1,1), period=12))
fit_211_011 <- Arima(y, order=c(2,1,1), seasonal=list(order=c(0,1,1), period=12))
fit_011_111 <- Arima(y, order=c(0,1,1), seasonal=list(order=c(1,1,1), period=12))
fit_111_110 <- Arima(y, order=c(1,1,1), seasonal=list(order=c(1,1,0), period=12))
fit_110_110 <- Arima(y, order=c(1,1,0), seasonal=list(order=c(1,1,0), period=12))

# Build comparison table -----------------------------------------------

model_comp <- tibble(
  model = c(
    "SARIMA(1,1,2)(0,1,1)[12]",
    "SARIMA(2,1,2)(0,1,1)[12]",
    "SARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]",
    "SARIMA(2,1,1)(0,1,1)[12]",
    "SARIMA(0,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]",
    "SARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,0)[12]",
    "SARIMA(1,1,0)(1,1,0)[12]"
  ),
  AIC = c(
    AIC(fit_112_011),
    AIC(fit_212_011),
    AIC(fit_111_111),
    AIC(fit_211_011),
    AIC(fit_011_111),
    AIC(fit_111_110),
    AIC(fit_110_110)
  ),
  BIC = c(
    BIC(fit_112_011),
    BIC(fit_212_011),
    BIC(fit_111_111),
    BIC(fit_211_011),
    BIC(fit_011_111),
    BIC(fit_111_110),
    BIC(fit_110_110)
  )
)

# Print table
model_comp

```

```{r}
final_sarima <- Arima(y,
                      order = c(1,1,2),
                      seasonal = list(order=c(0,1,1), period = 12),
                      include.constant = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# STEP 6: Diagnostic Checking ----------------------------------------

# 1. Residual time series plot
autoplot(residuals(final_sarima)) +
  ggtitle("Residuals of SARIMA(1,1,2)(0,1,1)[12]") +
  ylab("Residuals") +
  theme_minimal()

# 2. ACF of residuals
Acf(residuals(final_sarima),
    main = "ACF of Residuals – SARIMA(1,1,2)(0,1,1)[12]")

# 3. PACF of residuals (optional but useful)
Pacf(residuals(final_sarima),
     main = "PACF of Residuals – SARIMA(1,1,2)(0,1,1)[12]")

# 4. Ljung–Box test (lag = 24 recommended for monthly data)
Box.test(residuals(final_sarima),
         lag = 24,
         type = "Ljung-Box")

# 5. Normality check: QQ plot
qqnorm(residuals(final_sarima))
qqline(residuals(final_sarima), col = "red")

# 6. Histogram of residuals
hist(residuals(final_sarima),
     breaks = 40,
     main = "Histogram of Residuals",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "black")

```

```{r}
## STEP 8: Forecast the next 12 months --------------------------------------

library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)

# 12-month ahead forecast
cpi_forecast <- forecast(final_sarima, h = 12)

# Print forecast values
cpi_forecast

# Plot forecast
autoplot(cpi_forecast) +
  ggtitle("12-Month Forecast — CPI using SARIMA(1,1,2)(0,1,1)[12]") +
  xlab("Date") +
  ylab("CPI Index") +
  theme_minimal()

```
